name: vscode-documentdb
description: DocumentDB and MongoDB GUI: Connect to databases, run queries, and manage your data. Supports working with databases across both cloud-based and local environments.

scripts.clean: rimraf out dist coverage && npm run clean --workspaces --if-present
scripts.package:win32: npm run webpack-prod && cd dist && npm pkg delete "scripts.vscode:prepublish" && npx vsce package --no-dependencies --out ../%npm_package_name%-%npm_package_version%.vsix
scripts.package:default: npm run webpack-prod && cd dist && npm pkg delete "scripts.vscode:prepublish" && npx vsce package --no-dependencies --out ../${npm_package_name}-${npm_package_version}.vsix
scripts.package-prerelease:win32: npm run webpack-prod && cd dist && npm pkg delete "scripts.vscode:prepublish" && npx vsce package --pre-release --no-dependencies --out ../%npm_package_name%-%npm_package_version%-pre-release.vsix
scripts.package-prerelease:default: npm run webpack-prod && cd dist && npm pkg delete "scripts.vscode:prepublish" && npx vsce package --pre-release --no-dependencies --out ../${npm_package_name}-${npm_package_version}-pre-release.vsix
scripts.webpack-dev: rimraf ./dist && npm run webpack-dev-ext && npm run webpack-dev-wv
scripts.webpack-prod: rimraf ./dist && npm run webpack-prod-ext && npm run webpack-prod-wv
scripts.resapi.resetall: rimraf out node_modules dist && npm install && npm run resapi.link
scripts.l10n:auto: cross-env AZURE_TRANSLATOR_KEY= cross-env AZURE_TRANSLATOR_REGION= npx @vscode/l10n-dev generate-azure --outDir ./l10n ./l10n/bundle.l10n.json ./package.nls.json

//overrides: Pin transitive dependencies: glob 12 for latest, test-exclude 7 for compatibility

viewsWelcome.contents:
Welcome! To start exploring and managing your database, you'll need to connect to it first. Once connected, you can easily view, query, and modify your data.

[$(plus) Add New Connection…](command:vscode-documentdb.command.connectionsView.newConnection)
Click the button above to get started and unlock the full potential of your database.

Discover DocumentDB clusters across your cloud providers. Activate discovery to explore and manage your databases.
[$(search) Activate Service Discovery](command:vscode-documentdb.command.discoveryView.addRegistry)
Click the button above to get started and to start discovering your databases.

commands.title: Add To Connections In The "DocumentDB View"

commands.//: [Query Playground] Scan Collection Schema for field completions
commands.title: Query Playground: Scan Collection Schema

view/item/context.//: [TreeItem] Refresh Item (cluster, database, collection, documents, indexes) -> but not in azure(ResourceGroups|FocusView) as it's done there by the Azure Resources host extension.
[TreeItem] Refresh Item in the DiscoveryView (dynamic and context based)

documentDB.confirmations.showUrlHandling.description: Show confirmation dialogs when handling external URLs that open DocumentDB connections in VS Code.

documentDB.confirmations.confirmationSty.description: The behavior to use when confirming operations that cannot be undone, such as deleting resources.
enumDescriptions: Prompts with a warning dialog where you click a button to confirm deletion.
Prompts with a challenge where you select the correct number among multiple options to confirm deletion.
Prompts with an input box where you enter the name of the resource to confirm deletion.

documentDB.userInterface.ShowOperationSu.description: Show detailed operation summaries, displaying messages for actions such as database drops, document additions, deletions, or similar events.

documentDB.copyPaste.showLargeCollection.description: Show a warning dialog before copying large collections. When disabled, copy operations will proceed without any size-based warnings.

documentDB.copyPaste.largeCollectionWarn.description: The number of documents in a source collection that triggers a warning about potentially slow copy and paste operations. Set to a higher value to reduce warnings, or a lower value to see warnings for smaller collections.

documentDB.local.port.description: Port to use when connecting to DocumentDB Local instance

documentDB.playground.confirmRunAll.description: Show a confirmation dialog before running the entire playground file with Run All.

documentDB.batchSize.description: Number of documents to display per cursor iteration in the Query Playground and Interactive Shell. Applies to find(), aggregate(), and other cursor-returning operations.

documentDB.timeout.description: Maximum time (in seconds) allowed for a query to execute in the Query Playground or Interactive Shell before the operation is cancelled.

documentDB.shell.initTimeout.description: Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for the Interactive Shell to connect during initialization. Default value is 60 seconds.

documentDB.shell.display.colorOutput.description: Enable ANSI color-coded JSON output in the Interactive Shell. Disable for accessibility (screen readers) or piped output.

documentDB.shell.display.autocompletion.description: Enable autocompletion in the Interactive Shell (when available). Reserved for future use.

documentDB.collectionView.defaultPageSiz.description: Default page size for loading data in the collection view.

documentDB.experimental.enableAIQueryGen.markdownDescription:
Enable AI-powered natural language to query translation in the collection view. When enabled, you can type questions in plain language and have them translated into database queries.

Note: Experimental features might not work as expected, and AI-generated content might not always be accurate.

documentDB.aiAssistant.findQueryPromptPa.description: Path to a custom prompt template file for find query optimization. Leave empty to use the built-in template.

documentDB.aiAssistant.aggregateQueryPro.description: Path to a custom prompt template file for aggregate query optimization. Leave empty to use the built-in template.

documentDB.aiAssistant.countQueryPromptP.description: Path to a custom prompt template file for count query optimization. Leave empty to use the built-in template.

documentDB.aiAssistant.crossCollectionQu.description: Path to a custom prompt template file for cross-collection query generation. Leave empty to use the built-in template.

documentDB.aiAssistant.singleCollectionQ.description: Path to a custom prompt template file for single collection query generation. Leave empty to use the built-in template.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with DocumentDB
walkthroughs.description: Connect to any DocumentDB or MongoDB database and start managing data directly inside VS Code.

steps.title: 1. Open the DocumentDB Extension
steps.description: Click the DocumentDB icon in the sidebar to get started. This opens the database explorer panel.

steps.title: 2. Connect to a Database
steps.description: Use a connection string or sign in with your cloud provider to connect to **DocumentDB** or **MongoDB**

steps.title: 3. Browse Collections
steps.description: Explore your collections using Table, Tree, or JSON views. You can view documents with pagination support for large datasets.

steps.title: 4. Run Queries with Autocomplete
steps.description: Use the built-in editor to run find queries. Get syntax highlighting and field name suggestions while you type.

steps.title: 5. Use Azure Resources (optional)
steps.description: **If you use the Azure Resources extension**, you can now access Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB (RU) and Azure DocumentDB clusters from the Azure Resource View. This **optional integration** helps you work from the Azure environment you already use. [Read more...](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-documentdb/discussions/251)

steps.title: 6. Copy and Paste Collections
steps.description: Quickly copy and paste collections between databases or connections. Perfect for moving smaller datasets and ad-hoc transfers. [Read more...](https://aka.ms/vscode-documentdb-copy-and-paste)

x-announcedMigrationProviders.name: Azure DocumentDB Migration
x-announcedMigrationProviders.description: Assess and migrate your databases to Azure DocumentDB.
